lesson.title: Defer, Panic, Recover
lesson.summary: Schedule cleanup, handle crashes, and recover from panics.

sections.heading: Defer — cleanup that always runs
sections.bodyHtml:
defer schedules a function call to run when the surrounding function returns — whether it exits normally or panics. Use it for cleanup like closing files or unlocking mutexes:

f, err := os.Open("file.txt")
if err != nil {
    log.Fatal(err)
}
defer f.Close()
// work with f ...

Deferred calls run in LIFO order (last deferred runs first). Arguments are evaluated when the defer statement executes, not when the deferred call runs.

sections.heading: Panic — unexpected failures
sections.bodyHtml:
A panic stops normal execution and unwinds the stack, running deferred functions along the way. Use panic() for truly unrecoverable situations (not for normal error handling):

func init() {
    if err := setup(); err != nil {
        panic("setup failed: " + err.Error())
    }
}

Common panics: index out of bounds, nil pointer dereference, type assertion failure.

sections.heading: Recover — catching a panic
sections.bodyHtml:
recover() inside a deferred function regains control after a panic. It returns the panic value, or nil if no panic is happening:

func safeCall(fn func()) {
    defer func() {
        if r := recover(); r != nil {
            fmt.Println("recovered from:", r)
        }
    }()
    fn()
}

Use recover sparingly — typically at the top of a goroutine or in middleware. Let panics crash the program unless you have a good reason to catch them.

sections.heading: Your turn
sections.bodyHtml:
Open practice/defer-panic/main.go.

Write a function that uses defer to print "done" when it returns. Make it call panic("oops") and observe that the deferred print still runs.

questions.prompt: When does a deferred function execute?

options.label: When the surrounding function returns (or panics)

questions.prompt: What order do multiple deferred calls run in?
Where must recover() be called to catch a panic?

options.label: After the statement that caused the panic

exercises.title: Defer and panic
exercises.instructions: Write a main function that prints "start", defers "done", panics with "oops", and observe defer still runs.
